observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendation to the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the following officials to:

Municipal Accountant

To transfer in the books of accounts the road networks carried in the Registry of Public Infrastructure. If not possible, coordinate with the Municipal Engineer for the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

Maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the road and its components namely: a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures, and d) other miscellaneous structures.

Reconcile the subsidiary ledger of the road and its components with the Local Road Inventory and Road Map to ensure completeness.

Municipal Engineer

Segregate and recognize the cost of each component of the local roads with identified cost;

Determine the components of local roads identified in the inventory without corresponding cost and recognize the cost of each component at its fair value;

Provide the local accountant and the general services officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

Municipal General Services Officer

Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer;

Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. 

Inventory Committee

Conduct regular physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network system as basis in the reconciliation of the accounting records with the records of the Local Road Inventory and Road Map.
observations.managementAction:
We already made talks with MEO, MPDO and MGSO to account all the Local Roads of the Municipality and the MPDO started to draft its Road Map. 

However, due to pandemic, this was stopped and was not prioritize to give way to the immediate COVID-19 response programs, projects and activities. Nevertheless, we will comply with the above recommendations in CY 2021.

The MEO already prepared a lit with complete description of the local roads and submitted the Office of the Audit Team Leader a copy of the report, pending submission of a copy of the report to MAccO and MGSO
observations.reason: Not prioritize during the year due to COVID-19 response priorities and limited manpower due to skeleton COVID-19 infection and quarantine protocols

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to: 

Prepare a reconciliation schedule of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and adjusted on a timely basis; and

Perform and maintain review and appraisal of transactions to deter error and omissions, and to prevent the recurrence of this issue.
observations.managementAction: We already started tracing the transactions and we will immediately reconcile to correct the balances of the cited accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:

Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to secure from the Local Development Council and the Sangguniang Bayan an approved SAIP to support and/or cover the discussed medical supplies in the total amount of   P811,222.05.

Direct the Municipal Accounting Office to make the necessary adjustments relative to the charging of labor expenses from BGCM; and

Henceforth, comply with the requirements of laws, rules and regulations to support the National Government’s effort towards a unified, cohesive and orderly implementation of the national policy to address COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.managementAction: The labor is not a regular payroll but it pertains to labor for the construction of Decontamination Facilities. Nevertheless, we will secure the required approval of another SAIP.
observations.reason: Failed to secure approval

observations.managementAction: We doing our best to avoid delays and remit on time. In fact, we have assigned a Job Order dedicated to encoding ORs. Although, there are still few minimal delays in CY 2020 ranging from 1 to 4 days.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the GFPS to:

Institutionalize the GAD Database as spearheaded by the MPDO with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data including the result of the CBMS to be conducted in CY 2020 which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming, and policy formulation; 

Undertake comprehensive gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; and

Revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues.
observations.managementAction: On-going implementation of CBMS survey in order to generate sex-disaggregated data.
observations.reason: Already started the CBMS in CY 2019 but was interfered by the series of earthquakes then came the COVID-19 pandemic responses spearheaded by the MDRRMO and MSWDO who were the GAD Focal personnel then and now, respectively.

Revisit the Municipal Ordinances which provides necessary rules and regulations in the public market supervision and properly/strictly enforce the same especially on the surcharges/sanctions for delayed/ non-payment of rental fees; and

Initiate basic procedures to be performed upon the incurrence of delays in payment of the stall occupants to the actual cancellation/revocation of the lease contracts.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:

Review and properly analyze the nature of transaction before assigning an account to be used and to be guided with COA Circular No. 2015-009 for the Prescribed Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs since it defines and describes the detail on when to use a specific account; and

Reclassify the transfer from different barangays as Other Payables, Excess funds from LGSF as Due to NGAs and the PHIC funds for primary care expenses as to Due to PhilHealth accounts.

observations.recommendation:
In order to foster public awareness and monitoring of the procurement process and implementation of perfected and awarded contracts of the Municipal Government, we recommended the following:

Require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) for all types of procurement at the end of each semester and have it approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity; and

Submit the duly approved PMR to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester; copy furnished the Audit Team’s Office.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct:
The Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting journal entry to record all checks that are already stale, and thereafter record immediately all book reconciling items.
Request in writing with the depository bank copies of the needed Credit and Debit Memos as the basis for the recording of the adjustments in the books;
The Municipal Treasurer to make representations with the depository bank on unacted long outstanding bank reconciling items.
Ensure constant coordination between the Municipal Treasurer and Accounting Office for timely reconciliation of cash account balances at month-end.

Recommendation:
We recommended Management to require the immediate liquidation of the above cash advance and strictly enforce the rules and regulation on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to prevent unliquidated cash advances at year-end.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor cash advances and regularly remind /require the liquidation of a previous cash advance, or the proper accounting thereof, before another cash advance is granted to an accountable officer, and henceforth ensure the strict compliance to the Rules and Regulations on the Granting, Utilization and Liquidation of Cash Advance.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to exert all efforts to establish the completeness of the relevant data/ information on the PPEs and review the propriety of the computation of the depreciation, update the Lapsing Schedule of PPEs to present an accurate net carrying value of the PPEs, and prepare book adjustments for these accounts.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
Direct the GSO Head, in coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist, to include all the breeding stocks in the physical count plan to determine the existence and actual condition of the assets, especially those that have been long acquired;
To direct the Municipal Accountant to record adjustments in the books, if necessary, after reconciliation of records with the former offices, and to maintain an accounting schedule that provides sufficient details on each breeding stocks; and
Improve the monitoring and overseeing of the program to reach more recipients that will benefit the program including the update its monitoring report to include information such as but not limited to: (1) change of beneficiaries due to replacement, death, resignation, etc. including the reason for such changes; (2) number of live calves and piglets per pregnancy; (3) per cycle beneficiaries and status; and other information deemed relevant.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
Require the MHO personnel in charge of the liquidation to ensure that pertinent documents of transactions related to SRA payments are complete, proper and duly submitted to the required offices as mentioned in the above guidelines; and
Henceforth, to ensure strict adherence to pertinent laws, rules and regulations in granting benefits to personnel in order to maintain efficiency in the utilization of government funds and to avoid irregular, unnecessary, unconscionable, excessive, and extravagant expenditures that are disallowed in audit.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
Require the MDRRMO-Designate to submit immediately to the Municipal Accounting Office the duly signed COVID 19 cash assistance payroll to avoid issuance of the Notice of Suspension; and
Strengthen the internal controls in the disbursement process of the Municipality to ensure that all financial transactions paid are supported with complete documentation.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to observe complete disclosures for the collateral of financial liabilities in the Notes to Financial Statements particularly those required in the prevailing regulations of IPSAS 30.

Recommendation:	Henceforth, we recommend to the Municipal Mayor to:
Direct the MDRRMO to further enhance its procedures in preparing the MDRRMFIP including due coordination with the other relevant offices to present balances in the Plan consistent and reflective of the AIP and tally with accounting records to present a reliable Plan; and
To ensure that proper review and reconciliation be done before the finalization and approval of said Plan.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO to coordinate with each other for the monthly preparation and proper format of the Report of Sources and Utilization of the MDRRMF, accurate and validated amounts to be reported; and
Ensure the submission of the Report on or before 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor and the Regional Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (RDRRMC) through the Regional Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnish the Regional Offices of the DILG and DBM as required in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to henceforth ensure improvement in the preparation of the AIP through proper planning including consultation and deliberation with the officials concerned, specific identification of development projects that will actually address environmental management and socio-economic needs of the constituents, as required by the relevant laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the following:

The Municipal Health Officer (MHO) to:

Since the medical and other supplies, drugs and medicines and the like are being kept under their custody, it should prepare and maintain the required inventory forms, such as Stock Cards and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and a logbook that would show the description, the quantity, name and signature, and address of the recipients.

Upon receipt of inventory by the person-in-charge at the MHO, each item delivered and received should be recorded in individual Stock Cards which shall be updated regularly to monitor the existing balances as well as the expiry dates of these supplies. At the end of every month, a Summary of the Medical, Dental, Laboratory and Other Supplies, Drugs and Medicines should be prepared and submitted to MAO for recording and updating in the corresponding supplies ledger.

The Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) to record in the books the receipts and issuances/usage of such supplies, and henceforth, subject incoming supplies of similar nature to the same procedure as provided by the above-mentioned guidelines.
observations.managementAction:
Has assigned personnel for the recording and custody of the inventory of medical and other supplies received by the Office and the release/use/distribution thereof

The MAccO has recorded the receipts and issuances of medical and other supplies received and/or release/use/distributed every time the office is furnished by the MHO a copy of the PTR and quarterly inventory reports submitted by the MHO.

Since the fund was fully utilized in CY 2020 and no other Bayanihan Grant were issued to the LGU, the LGU found it unnecessary to create a Special Account and the opening of a new bank account thereof.

Henceforth, the LGU will comply to the specific requirements of the funding or grants.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to henceforth perform loan aging and analysis and maintain a record thereof to accurately identify and classify the Loans Payable account into current or non-current in accordance with the specific provisions in IPSAS and PD 1445.
observations.managementAction:
We have communicated this with our accounting software provider to request the inclusion of the segregation of the current and non-current portion of Loans Payable in our accounting reporting system. Various communications were made with the assigned programmer and they’re still working on it. 

We are also currently considering shifting to other accounting software to consider networking of the whole reporting units of the LGU. We also heard the plans of the Provincial Accountant on the use of one software in all LGUs in Davao del Sur liking it to Davao del Norte where all accounting systems of the LGUs were connected with the Provincial Accounting Software. We will also wait for the final instructions on these.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) to make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances and reconcile the same with the Municipal General Services Office (MGSO). Henceforth, we recommend that incoming purchases or acquisitions of supplies of similar nature to the same procedures as provided by the above-mentioned guidelines, and subsequently, record in the books the utilization and/or distribution of such supplies.
observations.managementAction: The recording purchases of supplies was previously recorded as inventory before. However, our assigned staff inadvertently short-cut the process of recording the purchases and issuances as direct expenses thinking that these inventories will then be expensed. Nevertheless, we will correct our recording practice.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to perform the necessary adjustments to record Depreciation Expense and the corresponding Accumulated Depreciation for School Buildings under the SEF. Henceforth, to ensure complete and correct recording of the depreciation expense to all depreciable assets of the Municipality in compliance to the above-mentioned Guidelines in the recognition of depreciation.

observations.recommendation:

Implement effective measures as identified in the Solid Waste Management Plan and specified in RA 9003 to minimize, if not to eliminate, the wastes presently stacked/dumped within in the Residual Containment Area pending the establishment, development and actual operation of the Sanitary Landfill;

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to discontinue the existing practice and require all collectors to turn-over/remit their collections daily and intact including those two collectors assigned at the field whose assignments are just located near the municipal hall;

Also, the schedule of making deposits of collections to the bank shall be made daily or not later than the next banking day; and

Require the Municipal Treasurer and the liquidating officer to properly review and verify all RCDs of collectors to ensure that all collections are properly accounted and deposits are turned-over/remitted intact to avoid government funds to risk of loss, theft, misuse and/or misappropriation.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

Ensure that PPAs allocated in the 70% calamity fund are those that will address disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery as enumerated in the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG while expenses for the normal operations of the agency shall be charged to the General Fund; and

Require the accounting and/or other concerned offices to immediately submit lacking vouchers duly suspended in audit to prevent maturity to disallowance.

We recommended that the Local Government of Malalag:

Direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant and the General Service Office to revisit the Chapter 7 of the NGAS Manual and strictly adhere to the rules and procedures in accounting for supplies and materials so as to come up with updated, reliable and accurate balances of inventory accounts, and to detect existence of supplies misusage, damage or losses due theft, and establish accountabilities thereof.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Government of Malalag, thru the Municipal Accountant, to establish and maintain a special account in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund as well as maintain complete set of subsidiary ledgers to ensure that the fund had been appropriated and disbursed only for the purpose/s for which they are intended, and to have adequate and reliable information as to its assets, liabilities and equity.

observations.managementAction: Already implemented in CY 2018

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality of Malalag:

Create the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO) to facilitate the achievement of the solid waste management goal of the Municipality.

Strictly impose the penalties and penal provisions indicated in the Municipal Ordinance #167 to discourage any prohibited acts related to solid waste management and encourage industries and households to take responsibilities in segregating their waste at source.

Coordinate with its 15 component barangays to issue/reiterate to its constituents the importance of adhering to the Municipal Ordinance, and the penalties and other consequences by violating the said Municipal Ordinance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the both the Office of the Municipal Accountant and the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to religiously observe timeliness in the remittance of the provincial share of the SEF collection, which is within 15 days of every succeeding month.
observations.managementAction: Directed the concerned offices to remit the SEF provincial share within 15 days of succeeding month.

Implement projects / programs / activities during the first and second quarters of the year.

To transfer in the books of accounts the road networks carried in the Registry of Public Infrastructure. If not possible, to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer for the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

Municipal Engineer
Segregate and recognize the cost of each component of the local roads with identified cost;
Determine the components of local roads identified in the inventory without corresponding cost and recognize the cost of each component at its fair value;
Provide the local accountant and the general services officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
Municipal General Services Officer
Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer;

Conduct regular physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network system as basis in the reconciliation of the accounting records with the records of the Local Road Inventory and Road Map.

Recommendations:

The Municipal Health Officer (MHO) to:
Since the medical and other supplies, drugs and medicines and the like are being kept under their custody, it should prepare and maintain the required inventory forms, such as Stock Cards and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and a logbook that would show the description, the quantity, name and signature, and address of the recipients.
Upon receipt of inventory by the person-in-charge at the MHO, each item delivered and received should be recorded in individual Stock Cards which shall be updated regularly to monitor the existing balances as well as the expiry dates of these supplies. At the end of every month, a Summary of the Medical, Dental, Laboratory and Other Supplies, Drugs and Medicines should be prepared and submitted to MAO for recording and updating in the corresponding supplies ledger.
The Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) to record in the books the receipts and issuances/usage of such supplies, and henceforth, subject incoming supplies of similar nature to the same procedure as provided by the above-mentioned guidelines.

Prepare a reconciliation schedule of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and adjusted on a timely basis; and

In order to foster public awareness and monitoring of the procurement process and implementation of perfected and awarded contracts of the Municipal Government, we recommended the following:

Require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) for all types of procurement at the end of each semester and have it approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity; and

Submit the duly approved PMR to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester; copy furnished the Audit Team’s Office.

We recommended to management the following:

The Local Chief Executive should create an Inventory Committee that will be responsible for the conduct of physical count.
Conduct the annual physical count of all the property, plant and equipment owned by the Municipality to establish the existence and correct the balances of PPE accounts;
Prepare promptly and completely the Report on the Physical Count of PPE.
Submit religiously the RPCPPE to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year.
Create an Appraisal Committee to appraise all properties which cost cannot be reliably determined due to unavailability of financial records. Require the Appraisal Committee to prepare an Appraisal Report and submit the same to the Commission on Audit for Technical Review and Inspection.

We also recommended that the General Service Office (GSO) and the Municipal Accountant shall completely maintain the prescribed Property Cards and PPELC, respectively and reconcile it with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of the PPE in the financial statements.

It is also noteworthy to say that accomplishing these recommendations is vital and significantly important as these would make the PPE accounts and the Financial Statements as a whole, reliable, accurate and fairly presented.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
In order to present an accurate and reliable balance of the Cash in Bank accounts in the books of General Fund, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund, we offered the following corrective measures:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank accounts and the related income and expenses accounts. Copies of the credit and debit memos shall be secured from the bank to support the adjustments.
We also recommended that the Bank Reconciliation Statements should be regularly prepared every month to preclude accumulation of reconciling items of both the bank and the books and obtain the correct balance if the Cash in Bank Accounts.  Accumulation of too many reconciling items defeats the purpose of providing the highest standards of honesty, consistency and objectivity in the keeping of books of accounts to safeguard against inaccurate and misleading financial information.
We further recommended to the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to determine the inactive bank accounts and cause the closure of these accounts to preclude the charges imposed by the banks.
Management’s Comments:
The Municipal Accountant duly noted the audit team’s recommendation and assured that matter will be acted upon and will coordinate with the team so as to duly effect and adjust the accounts affected.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Government of Malalag:
Direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant and the General Service Office to revisit the Chapter 7 of the NGAS Manual and strictly adhere to the rules and procedures in accounting for supplies and materials so as to come up with updated, reliable and accurate balances of inventory accounts, and to detect existence of supplies misusage, damage or losses due theft, and establish accountabilities thereof.
Conduct actual site inspection and/or physical inventory be conducted by the Municipality’s authorized committee of three to determine if there still existed substantial unused supplies and which the Committee’s Report may be used as basis in adjusting the Expense and Inventory accounts and/or recognizing the unused or unconsumed supplies and materials.
Henceforth, all purchases of supplies and materials should be taken up as Inventories under the appropriate account.
Management’s Comments:
The General Service Officer and the Municipal Accountant responded that the setting up of inventory accounts upon receipt of the inventories and immediately issuing it to end users only lengthened the process of inventory receipt and issuance.
COA’s Rejoinder:
Though the audit team understands the predicament of the Management, the team maintains its recommendations that inventory accounts should be maintained so as to establish the accountabilities of personnel in-charge in the custody, monitoring and recording of inventories.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive of the Municipality direct the responsible officials of the Municipality to:
Complete the annual physical inventory of the Property, Plant and Equipment;
Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for PPE;
Conduct reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the accounting records; and
Submit the RPCPPE to COA not later than January 31 of each year.
Management’s Comments:
During the exit conference, the management thru the Municipal GSO and Municipal Accountant pledged to complete the finalization of the report of physical inventory and to reconcile the same with the records of the accounting.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Government of Malalag to strictly adhere with the provisions and guidelines set forth in the COA Memorandum Circular No. 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012 and the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of the Republic Act No. 10121.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Coordinate with the MDRRMO in determining the variance and prepare necessary adjusting entries, as the case may be.
Closely coordinate with the MDRRMO in the determination of the remaining balances of the LDRRMF for the years 2013 to 2016 and set up the required individual subsidiary ledger for each year of transfer.
Coordinate with the MDRRMO and Municipal Treasurer’s Office in determining the remaining balance of the 2012 LDRRMF and reverts back to the unappropriated surplus in the General Fund book. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries.
Transfer the unexpended/unutilized balance of the CY 2017 appropriation for LDRRMF to the Trust Fund book, set up its separate SL, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries.

The LDRRMO secretariat and the Municipal Accountant were amenable with the audit team’s recommendations. It was agreed the coordination will be done to trace the unreconciled balances between each record.

Recommendations:

The Focal Person for the project avowed to check the documents the needed to support the transactions.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Government of Malalag re-evaluate its programs/projects under the 20% DF that shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.
Management’s Comments:
The Management maintained its stand in the relevance of the programs and projects it charged against the 20% development fund, stating that not all development projects are in the form of investment or capital expenditures.
COA’s Rejoinder:
The audit team sustains its audit recommendations to the management in re-evaluation of the programs and projects being charged against the 20% development fund. The team also critically emphasizes the provisions of the Item 2.3 of the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 which expressly stated that development projects charged against the development fund shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Government of Malalag, thru the Municipal Accountant, to establish and maintain a special account in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund as well as maintain complete set of subsidiary ledgers to ensure that the fund had been appropriated and disbursed only for the purpose/s for which they are intended, and to have adequate and reliable information as to its assets, liabilities and equity.
In addition, we also recommended that separate financial statements (Statement of Financial Position, Statement of Financial Performance and Statement of Cash Flows) should be prepared and submitted regularly to COA as provided by above-stated government laws, rules and guidelines.
Management’s Comments:
The management through the Municipal Accountant, assured the audit team to take appropriate actions on the audit observations.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal School Board carefully re-evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF Budget taking into consideration the specific PPAs set forth under Section 1 of RA No. 5447, Sections 100 (c) 272 of RA No. 7160, and the allowable expenses chargeable against the SEF enumerated in DepEd, DBM, DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017.
We also recommended to discontinue utilizing the Special Education Fund (SEF) for purposes/expenditures not specifically allowed by the abovementioned government laws, rules and regulations, otherwise, the same shall be disallowed in audit.
Management’s Comments:
The management through the co-chairman of the Municipal School Board assured that the matter will be discussed among the members of the school board in its upcoming sessions. The prohibitions enumerated in the DepEd, DBM, DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017 will be discussed and considered accordingly as well.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Government of Malalag that the MDRRMO, Municipal Budget Office, and the Municipal Planning and Development Office closely coordinate with each other in order to strictly comply with the guidelines provided in the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1, and be able to integrate the programs and activities for the LDRRM in the Annual Investment Program (AIP) of the Municipality before its final approval.
Management’s Comments:
The LDRRMO Secretariat was amenable with the audit team’s observation and recommendations. It the succeeding budget year of 2018, it was assured by the LDRRMO Secretariat that LDRRMFIP will be integrated and will formed as an integral part of the Municipal’s Annual Investment Plan.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipality of Malalag:
Create the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO) to facilitate the achievement of the solid waste management goal of the Municipality.
Strictly impose the penalties and penal provisions indicated in the Municipal Ordinance #167 to discourage any prohibited acts related to solid waste management and encourage industries and households to take responsibilities in segregating their waste at source.
Coordinate with its 15 component barangays to issue/reiterate to its constituents the importance of adhering to the Municipal Ordinance, and the penalties and other consequences by violating the said Municipal Ordinance.
Stop operating the controlled dumpsite in Brgy. Tagansule to prevent potential loss of properties and lives due to natural calamities and contamination, and expedite the processes in acquiring the new location for Sanitary Landfill.
Management’s Comments:
The management was amenable with the audit team’s recommendation. As discussed, more efforts will be made to expedite the acquisition of new site for the planned Sanitary Landfill. It was also agreed that Municipal Government will tap its 15 component barangay to effectively impose the Municipal Ordinance related to solid waste management.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Government of Malalag to integrate the provisions and requirements of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 as among the functions of the Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC). An issuance of another Executive Order amending the functions of the LCPC may serve the purpose.

Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC)
Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC)
Local State of Children’s Report (LSCR)
Children’s Profile

The management admitted that they were not able to prepare the above-listed reports and was not able to maintain the Children’s Profile. For the succeeding year, the management, through the LCPC Secretariat assured that the reports and profile will be provided and maintained.

observations.recommendation: Recommendations:  We recommended that the Accountant determine, analyze and reconcile the reciprocal accounts to avoid inaccurate and misleading information. The necessary adjusting entries should then be made.
Recommendation: We recommended to Management to submit the required report (RPCPPE) in accordance with the provisions of the Manual on the NGAS.

Recommendations:We recommended that all equipment related to disaster activities be bookedup under the “Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment” account. Also. Maintainsubsidiary ledgers required under COA Circular No. 2015-009.

Recommendation:We recommended that while the system cannot generate a financial statement showing the current and the non-current portion of liabilities, in the meantime, the Management shall prepare it manually to arrive at a PPSAS-compliant financial statement.

Recommendations:We recommended to Management to review properly the figure presented in the Cash FlowStatements vis-a-vis the figures presented in the Notes to Financial Statements to avoid misinterpretation on the part of the reader. The figures presented in all components of the financial statements must be appropriately presented in its designated caption.

Recommendation:We recommended to Management to observe the requirement of the PPSAS in the preparation and presentation of financial statements. Notes to Financial Statements is one of the components of the financial statements, hence, must be prepared and presented accurately and completely.

Recommendations:We recommended the following courses of action:
Fast track the completion of the projects. Regularly monitor the status of implementation; and
Before entering into another MOA, the Management see to it that it has the capacity to implement the project within the time agreed upon by evaluating first all the aspects of the projects including procurement activities and personnel capacity to avoid delays in the implementation.

Recommendation:We recommended that the Management re-evaluate its programs/projects under the 20% DF that shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures. Expenditures that are administrative in nature should be charged under the General Fund.

Recommendations:  We recommended the following:
The Municipality evaluate its capability in responding to calamities or disasters. Procure basic rescue and response equipment that will suit the kind of calamity or disaster that usually occurred or experienced by the Municipality. Utilize the unspent LDRRMF by incorporating it in the Municipality’s LDRRMFIP.
Preparethe Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis using the prescribed form under COA Circular 2012-02. This report shall be submitted by LDRRMO on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and LDC to the COA auditor.
The Management assured that they will comply with the recommendations.
The Audit Team, however, reminded Management that this is a reiteration of last year’s audit recommendation and should thus be complied with.

Recommendations: In order to foster public awareness and monitoring of the procurement process and implementation of perfected and awarded contracts of the Municipal Government, we recommended the following:
1. Require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) for all types of procurement at the end of each semester and have it approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity; and
2. 	Submit the duly approved PMR to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester, copy furnished the Audit Team’s Office.

We recommended that the unspent P3,846,036.50  LDRRMF be recorded as Trust Liabilities under the account “Trust Liabilities- Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” as provided by COA Circular Nos. 2012- 002 and 2015-009. Likewise, maintain the  subsidiary ledgers required by said COA Circular No. 2015-009.

Instruct the Accounting Department that  purchase  of food supplies, regardless  of  whether  or  not they  are consumed  within  the  accounting  period, shall be recorded   as  inventory following   the   perpetual inventory method.  Under the perpetual inventory method, an inventory control account is maintained in the General Ledger on a current basis. In addition, detailed inventory records are maintained for each inventory item.

         To avoid maintenance of inventory accounts of consumed food supplies, the GSO or Local Treasurer, shall consolidate weekly the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) for which supplies were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI). The SMMI together with the original copy of the RIS shall be submitted to the Chief Accountant as basis of recording the consumed food supplies as expense.
observations.reason: There were other purchases recorded directly to expense account.

observations.recommendation:
The management should strictly adhere to the provisions provided in the Joint Circular No. 2015-01 of   Commission on Audit, Department of Budget and Management Department of Interior and Local Government, Governance Commission of GOCCs and Department of National Defense, in the granting, liquidation and reporting of confidential expenses.

          Prepare an adjusting entry to set up a receivable for the cash advances made during the CY 2015 pertaining to confidential expenses, under the account “Advances to Special Disbursing Officer” and drop it from the books only upon receipt   by the Agency Accountant   the copy of transmittal letter, duly received by the ICFAU authorized staff.

We recommend that the agency should strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-010 as to the proper accomplishment of PO. The Municipal Treasurer, being the General Services Officer of the Municipality should see to it that PO must be properly accomplished. Submit   PO to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should   ensure that the procuring entity abides by the standards set forth by R.A 9184 and it’s IRR. Plan carefully the procurement activities and processes to avoid splitting of contracts. The BAC and the Accountant shall see to it that complete and proper documentation relating to procurement activities   must be compliant with COA Circular 2012-011, which prescribes the Guidelines and Documentary Requirement for Common Government Transactions to avoid suspension in audit.

             Settle the Notice of Suspension within the period prescribed in Section 82 of P.D. No. 1445.
We recommended that the management will establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System as required under Section 5.1 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.
We recommended that management should plan well all the aspects of project implementation, like the procurement processes as well as the technical aspects, to avoid unnecessary delays.
observations.reason: Only minimal targets were not attained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management that said Notices of Suspension be complied within the prescribed period, which is 90 days upon receipt, otherwise, shall become a disallowance pursuant to Section 82 of P.D. No. 1445. We also recommend to the management to examine first the required attachments or documentations before submitting the disbursement voucher to avoid suspension. Finally, reference may be had to COA Circular 2012-001which is the Revised Guidelines and Documentary Requirements for Common Government transactions.

Direct the GSO to prepare an Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and furnish the  Accounting Division a copy of it to be used as basis in drawing a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to transfer the subject properties to   “ Other Assets” account.
Apply immediate disposal of all properties  declared by the agency as obsolete or unserviceable.

Establish the Municipality’s GFPS in accordance with the composition prescribed in item 4.0 (A) (2)   of the JMC # 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA. 
Direct the Local Planning and Development Office to spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation. observations.recommendation:
Undertake programs, projects or activities consistent with the provision of Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan.
Evaluate the agency’s capability in responding calamity. Procure the best suited basic rescue and response equipment depending on the nature of disaster usually occurring in the locality. Reference may be had with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 for the type of disaster and the type of equipment.
Organize, train, equip and supervise the Local emergency response teams and Accredited Community Disaster Volunteers (ACDVs), ensuring that humanitarian aid workers are equipped with basic skills to assist mothers to breastfeed.
Develop, strengthen and operationalize mechanism for partnership or networking with private sectors, Civil Society Organizations (CSOs) and volunteer groups. 
Maintain a database of human resource, equipment, directories, and location of critical infrastructures and their capacity such as hospitals and evacuation centres.
Display hazard maps on prominent places.
All Equipment and other facilities acquired for rescue operation should be booked up as “Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment” as required under item 5.1.7 of COA Circular # 2012-002.
Municipal Accountant must draw adjusting entry (ies) effecting the transfer of the above unexpended balance of QRF   to Trust Liability- DRRM in the trust fund books.
The amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF should be discussed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
	
The Municipality shall annually observe GAD planning and budgeting and incorporates it in all its programming and budgeting exercises. The GAD activities
 in the GAD plan and budget must be included in the agency budget proposal
in accordance with the budget call. Agency  shall ensure that the cost of
implementing the GAD activities is part of its approved budget. At least five
percent (5%) of the total agency budget appropriations as authorized under the
 Annual Appropriations Ordinance, shall correspond to activities supporting 
GAD. Agency head shall be responsible for ensuring that GAD activities are provided with adequate resources. 

2.	The Municipality, through GAD Focal Point, prepares the annual GAD plan and budget and the annual GAD accomplishment report following the format prescribed by the Joint Circular 2012-02 of PWC, NEDA and DBM.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that management should formulate marketing plans and strategies to encourage businessmen and investors to utilize the new public market building in order to generate income that could be used for its operation.  Institute measures to protect possible damages and losses on the new public market.

Furthermore, the Local Government of Malalag must be more innovative in initiating a move to recover the cost of the Public Market Construction and any recovered amount should be recorded in the Local Government Unit’s General Fund.
observations.reason: Not implemented | Proponents on the construction of the New Public Market did not formulate marketing plans and strategies in encouraging  businessmen and investors to utilize the said building.
